name: Brunei
excerpt: The Sultanate of Brunei's influence peaked between the 15th and 17th centuries, when its control extended over coastal areas of northwest Borneo and the southern Philippines.

quick_facts.terrain: flat coastal plain rises to mountains in east; hilly lowland in west
quick_facts.major_exports: refined petroleum, natural gas, crude petroleum, hydrocarbons, fertilizers (2023)

summaries.geography_summary: close to vital sea lanes through South China Sea linking Indian and Pacific Oceans; two parts physically separated by Malaysia; the eastern part, the Temburong district, is an exclave and is almost an enclave within Malaysia
summaries.history_summary: The Sultanate of Brunei's influence peaked between the 15th and 17th centuries, when its control extended over coastal areas of northwest Borneo and the southern Philippines. Internal strife over royal succession, colonial expansion of European powers, and piracy subsequently brought on a period of decline. In 1888, Brunei became a British protectorate; independence was achieved in 1984. The same family has ruled Brunei for over six centuries, and in 2017, the country celebrated the 50th anniversary of Sultan Hassanal BOLKIAH’s accession to the throne. Brunei has one of the highest per-capita GDPs in the world, thanks to extensive petroleum and natural gas fields.
summaries.government_summary: absolute monarchy or sultanate. Independence: 1 January 1984 (from the UK)
summaries.economy_summary: almost exclusively an oil and gas economy; high income country; expansive and robust welfare system; the majority of the population works for the government; promulgating a nationalized halal brand; considering establishment of a bond market and stock exchange

suggested_reading: CIA World Factbook data (archived via factbook.json) | https://github.com/factbook/factbook.json

prayer_and_mission.prayer_intro: Brunei is an Islamic sultanate where Sharia law is enforced and Christian witness faces real legal restriction. Pray for the small Christian minority to remain faithful and for the gospel to continue quietly advancing.
prayer_points: Pray for protection and endurance for Brunei's small Christian community.
Pray for open doors for gospel witness despite significant legal restrictions on evangelism.
Pray for laborers willing to serve in a historically closed nation.
prayer_and_mission.mission_emphasis: Brunei's status as an Islamic sultanate with enforced Sharia law makes it a genuinely difficult field for open gospel witness. Ongoing needs include discreet support for the small existing Christian community and continued prayer for open doors.
